page.content: The table presents data comparing Trinity assembly and Digital normalization, Oases-Velvet, across various metrics. The total fragments for both methods are 87,209,130 (100.00%). For aligned concordantly 0 times, Trinity assembly shows 23,101,349 (26.49%), while Digital normalization has 35,552,373 (40.77%). In aligned concordantly 1 time, Trinity assembly has 28,325,506 (32.48%) compared to 30,552,963 (35.03%) for Digital normalization. There are no values reported for the row of reads aligning concordantly or discordantly 0 times. In aligned discordantly 1 time, Trinity assembly records 1,826,073 (7.90%), while Digital normalization significantly increases to 7,539,150 (21.21%). The total mates are 42,550,552 for Trinity assembly and 56,026,446 for Digital normalization.

page.content: For aligned 0 times, Trinity assembly indicates 24,594,154 (57.80%) while Digital normalization shows 31,100,316 (55.51%). In aligned exactly 1 time, Trinity assembly has 4,660,328 (10.95%), and Digital normalization presents 11,909,789 (21.26%). The aligned >1 times category reveals Trinity assembly at 13,296,070 (31.25%) and Digital normalization at 13,016,341 (23.23%). Finally, the overall alignment rate is reported as 85.90% for Trinity assembly and 82.17% for Digital normalization, illustrating the comparative performance of these assembly methods based on the provided data.
